question.text: If an alternative basis center's polled price is used when the main center's price is unavailable on expiry day, this is:

answer.text: An acceptable fallback methodology prescribed by exchanges like NCDEX subject to certain conditions
Only done with explicit SEBI approval on a case-by-case basis

explanation.text: **The Logic:** On NCDEX, if polled price on expiry day (E) for the main basis center is not available, the alternate basis center's polled price will be considered, subject to certain conditions. **The Trap:** It is permitted (not prohibited). It applies to all commodities, not just agricultural. Exchanges have pre-defined circular-based rules — no case-by-case SEBI approval needed. **Cross-Exam Context:** FSP fallback mechanisms are unique to commodity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Multiple basis centers ensure robustness of settlement price discovery — even if the primary market is disrupted, settlement can proceed with alternate center prices.
